displayTitle: The Survey Protects You — The Report Proves It

beats.narration: Your survey meter protects you in the moment you read it. The report is what proves the survey ever happened.

payload.title: THE REPORT IS THE PROOF
payload.sub: A survey nobody recorded earns no compliance credit.

beats.narration: Ten CFR 34.49 sets the surveys. Survey device and guide tube while approaching after each exposure, at every source exchange, and going into storage.

chips.s: Device and guide tube, while approaching
Confirm the source sits in the shield
Truck, darkroom, or vault — survey it in

beats.narration: When that storage survey ends the workday, 34.85 makes it a written record — kept three years. Field stations keep their compliance file on site.

tiles.s: Latest records stay at the jobsite

beats.narration: A defensible report identifies the job, source, device, and calibrated meter — then every reading with its units, its location, an area sketch, and your signature.

payload.heading: Anatomy of a record that holds up
payload.note: EVERY READING: A NUMBER, UNITS, AND A LOCATION

beats.narration: So record what the meter read, never what you expected. An honest gap is a procedure problem. An invented number is a federal violation.

payload.sub: If it isn't recorded, it didn't happen.
